2. Non working refrigerator (had to get it fixed twice).
3. Items took a long time to arrive.
4. When we mentioned all this to the receptionist at check out, she replied ‘did you tell the reception?’ At that point, we just couldn’t be bothered talking to a brick wall. Please train your front desk staff properly when handling complaints.

reviews.title: All road access to Marriott  KL closed New Year Eve
reviews.text:
I reached Kuala Lumpur Bukit Bintang Road at 300pm and the roads whole area was closed for New Year celebration. I drove in circles until 600pm and the roads leading to Marriott hotel were fully closed. The radio announced that all roads there are closing till 2am tomorrow and there is no way I can drive to Marriot hotel.
I phoned Marriot phone 3 times and the line was busy and no one responded.
On booking, there was no warning at all of the road closure. Marriot also never emailed nor phoned me warning of the full road closure.
I have contacted Agoda to assist as it was unfair for my money to be forfeited when there was no feasible way for me to reach Marriot hotel grounds before 2am the next day
The hotel refused and offered late check in till 11pm. The hotel also refused refund saying the government social media has informed the public. Marriott itself has not emailed nor phoned me to advise about this! 
This is a marquee branded hotel and not a one star lodging in Chow Kit and I find this rather unacceptable

reviews.title: Lousy hotel! Zero star hotel ever!
reviews.text:
This stay was totally unpleasant stay for me and my family.  

Firstly, I want to complain about the cleanliness of hotel room. When I reach hotel room, I can see the big dust flying in the air, toilet bowl was not clean still got hair inside the toilet bowl and the bath tub has dirty stain which I still need to wash with my own hand! Seem like the My hotel Room was vacant for some time.

Secondly, there is a strange sound from the toilet, after we checking, notice the sound is caused by air conditioning. We called to service counter and they sent technician to come and fix but no offer to change room even we request for it.
Which I think before handover the room to guest, hotel management should QC the room condition.

Thirdly, sound proof system of the room was unacceptable lousy! We can heard the door open and close sound from other room. This is not the worst, the worst case is the opposite room was making deadly noise since noon until midnight 4am. We call to desk for complain and the duty manager do warning the guest but til 4am the guest wasn’t stop their laughing noise still. I total complain the noisy noise for 2 times at 11pm and 4am. I feel myself was in a hostel rather than 5 stars hotel. It was ruin my holiday and money spend on your hotel. I need to bare the noise for whole staying, really like my student time staying in hostel and my next door make noise but I couldn’t do much of thing. 

Last but the least, when we checked out and complain to the staff, he just say sorry that’s all and seem like no plan to report to the management which totally disappointed us.

In the nutshell, I would like to rate zero star for JW Marriott hotel for room and services! This is the bad ever hotel stay in my life! 

Two word to conclude: dissatisfied & disappointed.

reviews.title: Bait & switch...beware
reviews.positives:
Housekeeping staff always did a good job
Location is central to KL
reviews.negatives:
Overall this is one of the more poorly run Marriott hotels I've stayed at. I would not stay here again. 

I booked a deluxe room, but was told by the check-in staff that if I wanted to get any rest, I would need to upgrade to a different room since all the deluxe rooms are on the 2nd floor and the noise from the street would be too loud for sleeping. Why would a classy hotel even have rooms that you can't sleep in? It just felt like a scam.

I sent my laundry outside for cleaning since the hotel wanted to charge me over $100 USD to do two loads of laundry. Another obvious scam. That laundry was delivered the next day, but the hotel didn't inform us until 24 hours later. Apparently this happens all the time, according to the laundry service I used.

Pillows on the bed were way too soft. No neck support and rather uncomfortable. 

The hotel is overly crowded and noisy. People let their kids run up and down the halls screaming and yelling all day long.

 The elevator is slow and often full, so it takes a long time to get to and from your floor.

The WiFi is slow and often kicks you off for no apparent reason. This happens multiple times a day. If you require a decent internet connection for work, you will want to choose a different hotel.

reviews.title: JW Marriott Bukit Bintang has the most baffling elevator logic I’ve encountered!
reviews.positives: Nice rooms, nice swimming pool, and the location is great, if you need to be in that location (otherwise traffic to get to and leave from can be a hassle).
reviews.negatives:
- As someone who spends over 200 nights a year in hotels across Asia, I’ve stayed at this property some 15-20 times. Yet, every visit, I find myself frustrated by the elevators. For example, you can be waiting on a floor while two lifts sit idle just below you, unmoving. You wait…and wait—only for an elevator from several floors away to finally arrive. This isn’t an isolated incident; after spending just a short time here, you’ll quickly realize how inefficient the entire lift system is.
- The check-in tablets seem ancient and they dont work properly.
- The rain-shower in my room was splashing water all across the bathroom.
- Breakfast buffet does not serve cold cuts

reviews.title: The dining location and experience can be far better than it is now.
reviews.positives: The room is nice, spacious and clean.  The receptionist (Ms Akari) who handled our check-in and check-out process has been awesome.
reviews.negatives:
We were disappointed with the breakfast and the overall dining experience. The venue, located on the lower ground floor, felt dim and enclosed, which made the dining experience less appealing. The main issue, however, was the layout. Although the breakfast spread was reasonable, the egg station and roti canai station, clearly the most popular spots, were placed in a far corner, squeezed beside the salad counter. This created a constant bottleneck, making it inconvenient for guests who simply wanted salad without joining the long queue.

Additionally, the “chefs” at both stations appeared inattentive and visibly unhappy on both days. While I can understand they might have been working over the Hari Raya holiday, this level of service is certainly not what one would expect from a Marriott hotel.

As an additional note, Luk Yu Chinese Restaurant was a disappointment. In fact, it felt like a step backward compared to the Luk Yu at AC Hotel by Marriott in Ipoh, where we previously had a much better experience, even though that hotel is of a lower grade than the JW Marriott Kuala Lumpur.

reviews.title: Not up to aspected
reviews.text:
My stay was not as great as I expected from a 5-star hotel.

Upon arrival:

The security staff was not very welcoming. We arrived in a small car, and the security repeatedly asked us to move forward to make space for a bigger car (a Vellfire at that time), while I was waiting for my husband to complete the check-in process.

We asked for directions to the basement parking, but we were given incorrect directions which led us to a one-way road.

When we arrived at our room, there was no dustbin provided. I called the receptionist since there was no direct extension number to housekeeping. However, after 30 minutes there was still no response, and I had to make a second call.

Soap was provided on the first day and we used it while enjoying the bathtub, but during our 2-night stay, the soap was never replenished on the second day.

Overall, I felt that the service did not meet the expectations of a 5-star hotel experience.

reviews.text:
Overall a very dissapointing stay. We booked 2 club rooms. And the club lounge is very dissapointing. The level of service you expect in the lounge is just not there. For simple things like tea tarik and hot chocolate at lunch time was too big of an ask. Some days I was told they didnt have it and others days i was told i would need to pay for it and it would be chargeable.
I thought this was ridiculous as you can request it during dinner. One of the days i asked for a hot chocolate and tea tarik. I waited 15 mins to be served barely warm chocolate. The waiter didnt even bother telling me they wouldnt serve the tea tarik until my dad went up to him and asked him how long it would be. When my dad prompted the waiter to inform me as well as i am the one who ordered it. He told me he thought i didnt need it anymore since i had been given a hot chocolate and that they only did tea made with tea bags. 
In the dinner service I also asked about mocktails as i dont drink alcohol and they offer cocktails. I got told its not something they do but they can make me something. But when the waiter brought the drink he also told me they dont normally do mocktails. This seemed to be an issue depending on who you asked.
Overall the service and whether you would get what you wanted would rely heavily on the server you asked. This was a pitiful excuse for a lounge and for the money i spent i wish i went to another hotel like the intercontinental, impiana or any other 5 star hotels.

reviews.title: A disappointing stay: More 3-star than 5-star
reviews.text:
The lobby gave us a warm welcome, setting high expectations for a five-star experience. Unfortunately, that excitement quickly faded upon entering our room. The air-conditioning vents were filled with dust, the room decor felt cold and uninspired—more like a three-star hotel than a luxury stay.

The lift system was a nightmare. With too few elevators for the number of guests, wait times were excessively long. Crowd control was poorly managed, leading to people cutting the queue and packing into the lifts just to avoid waiting even longer.

The biggest disappointment, however, was the food. Despite sharing a restaurant with The Ritz-Carlton and being located in Starhill Gallery Mall, the dining experience was abysmal. The dinner area lacked warmth and ambiance, making it feel uninviting. To make matters worse, guests have to walk about five minutes from the hotel lobby, passing through an awkward waiting area with mismatched sofas and a dingy bar.

The food itself was unacceptable—bland, repetitive, and completely uninspiring. We ended up leaving on the first night to eat elsewhere because the options were so limited and tasteless. It was one of the worst dining experiences I’ve ever had, especially at a so-called five-star hotel.

Overall, this stay felt more like a mediocre three-star experience rather than the luxury we expected. It simply did not live up to its brand reputation.

reviews.title: 5 star hotel with 3 star standard
reviews.text:
Stayed at JW Marriott Kuala Lumpur with high expectations, but the experience fell short of what a 5-star hotel should offer.

Basic amenities were missing, no bathrobes were provided in the room, and there was no “Make My Room” door hanger, which made housekeeping coordination inconvenient. The TV system also does not allow connection to mobile devices, so guests are limited to whatever channels are available, which feels outdated for a hotel of this calibre.

The lifts were constantly crowded, making peak hours frustrating and time-consuming. While the staff were polite, the service did not feel warm or sincere, more procedural and forced rather than genuinely hospitable. Front desk was slow.

On the positive side, the location is excellent and very convenient, and the breakfast spread was good with a wide variety of options. We also made use of their express checkout which was convenient.

Overall, the stay was underwhelming and did not justify the 5-star branding. There are better options in Kuala Lumpur, and I would choose to stay elsewhere next time.

reviews.title: Disappointing Experience at the Executive Lounge
reviews.text:
I recently visited the Executive Lounge, expecting a high level of professionalism and hospitality. Unfortunately, my experience was marred by the behavior and attitude of one of the lounge executive officers, Lisa.

From the very beginning, Lisa's demeanor was unwelcoming and unfriendly. Her tone of voice was harsh and lacked any warmth or politeness expected in such a setting. 

What stood out most was her inability to communicate effectively. Instead of offering assistance or providing clear guidance, her responses were dismissive and vague, leaving me feeling frustrated and unwelcome. Such behavior leaves a lasting bad impression on guests and tarnishes the reputation of the establishment.

The role of an Executive Lounge officer is to create a pleasant and accommodating atmosphere for guests, but Lisa's attitude was the complete opposite. It was evident that she lacked the necessary training in customer service and did not understand the importance of treating guests with respect and courtesy.

I strongly urge the management to address this issue by providing additional training to Lisa and ensuring that all staff members understand the importance of maintaining a professional and friendly attitude. The Executive Lounge is a premium facility, and guests expect premium service, which was sadly missing in this case.

I hope this feedback will be taken seriously, as improving the quality of service is crucial to maintaining guest satisfaction and loyalty.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text: We stayed at the Marriott Hotel in KL in July 2009. The hotel is in a fantastic location and is within easy walking distance of all the best shopping malls, cafes and restaurants in Bukit Bintang. Its also connected to an up-market shopping mall called the Star Hill Gallery. The room is well furnished with a comfortable bed and clean linens and towels. The room in quite small for a KL hotel and the bathroom even smaller. Our main complaint however was with the service. Although the hotel was busy and filled to near full capacity, there is no excuse for the poor service  of the staff. It took more than 45 minutes for our bags to arrive after we had checked in. On calling the house keeping, no one answered the phone. We tried reception and room service with same result. It took us more than half an hour to get someone on the phone to order food!! During check-out, it took another 35 minutes for our bags to arrive from the room which almost resulted in us missing our flight! This level of service is not expected at a Marriott! Overall its a decent hotel with a great location but very poor service. There are many other hotels in the area which offer better rooms and service at a lower cost.

reviews.text:
We stayed  at Executive room with lounge access n regretted . Firstly, we were denied entry twice when we wanted to have our breakfast. The lady staff asked us for  room number and.wihout checking , deny entry at the door.  When we persisted,, counter staff check & again denied. We had to pull out a letter to show them.  no apology. Staff  lounge need to be more courteous  and cheerful. Atmosphere. was  solemn n unfriendly., breakfast spread was minimal.. We even had to  get own napkin and cutlery as the staff did not make any  effort to provide service. We had to clear our plates and put it on the next table as the service was slow. Ironically, there were less than 20 persons in the lounge. After that first experience at lounge , we decided to have our breakfast at Shiook. It was so much better and staff were all Smiles . Food spread was definitely better too. 
If ever we decide to stay at Marriot again, we will definitely not waste money on the accommodation for club lounge access.

reviews.title: Memorable First Time Visit at Kuala Lumpur
reviews.positives:
My daughter and I recently spent three nights at JW Marriott Kuala Lumpur at Bukit Bintang, and our stay was made truly memorable by Nijhum at the reception, whose hospitality went far beyond anything we expected.

From the moment we checked in, Nijhum took genuine care of us. She kindly arranged complimentary breakfast for both of us for our entire stay, which was such a warm and unexpected gesture. 

Throughout our trip she helped us arrange taxis, guided us to sensible and good-value shopping areas, recommended places worth visiting, and pointed us towards wonderful local food. As a mother travelling alone with my daughter in an unfamiliar city, having someone so attentive, kind, and trustworthy at the front desk gave me real peace of mind.

This level of warmth and personal attention is rare. Nijhum represents the very best of what hospitality should be, and she is a real asset to the JW Marriott team. The hotel itself was excellent — clean, comfortable, and beautifully located — but it was Nijhum’s personal care that elevated our experience from a pleasant stay into a memorable one. 

Thank you, Nijhum. We will not forget your kindness, and we will certainly return.
